regions.name: Aegean Coast
regions.tagline: Olive groves, Greek islands, the most relaxed corner of Turkey.
regions.summary: The west-coast strip from Izmir down past Kuşadası to the Bodrum peninsula and Marmaris. Whitewashed villages, swimmable bays you can reach without a tour boat, fresh fish lunches, and the ruins of Ephesus a short drive from any of it. Think 'Greek islands but better food'.
regions.whenToGo: May–June and September–October (perfect weather, manageable crowds). July–August is hot and packed but the sea is at peak temperature.
highlights: Ephesus — the most complete Roman city in the Mediterranean, 30 minutes from Kuşadası
Yalıkavak's marina + windmill ridge — Bodrum peninsula's quietest evening
Şirince village — wine, pottery, and the slow Aegean afternoon
Çeşme + Alaçatı — wind-surfer's coast, hipster cafés, the Aegean's gentrified corner
itinerary: Day 1–3: Izmir (gateway), day-trip to Ephesus + Şirince
Day 4–5: Kuşadası or Selçuk base, deeper Ephesus + Pamukkale day trip
Day 6–8: Bodrum peninsula (Yalıkavak / Bitez), beach + boat days
Day 9–10: Marmaris if continuing south, or fly out from Bodrum

regions.name: Mediterranean Riviera (Turquoise Coast)
regions.tagline: Beaches with Roman ruins on top of them, the Lycian Way, the bluest water in Turkey.
regions.summary: From Antalya east to Alanya and west to Fethiye, the Turquoise Coast is the Mediterranean at its most photogenic. Lycian-era ruins built on cliffs above swimming coves, paragliding off Babadağ over Ölüdeniz, the gentle pace of Kaş.
regions.whenToGo: April–May or late September–October (warm enough to swim, no peak crowds). June–August for full beach season.
highlights: Kaputaş Beach near Kaş — postcard turquoise cove
Ölüdeniz lagoon + paragliding from Babadağ
Kaleiçi old town in Antalya — Ottoman lanes, Roman walls, harbor views
Aspendos amphitheater — still hosts opera in summer
Kayaköy ghost village — abandoned 1923, eerie at sunset
itinerary: Day 1–3: Antalya (Kaleiçi base), day-trip to Aspendos / Termessos
Day 4–5: Drive west to Kaş (3 hrs along Lycian coast)
Day 6–8: Fethiye + Ölüdeniz, paragliding + Saklıkent canyon
Day 9–10: Bodrum or fly out from Dalaman

regions.name: Cappadocia & Central Anatolia
regions.tagline: Fairy chimneys, underground cities, balloon dawns, and the Sufi heart of Turkey.
regions.summary: Volcanic landscape sculpted into surreal rock formations, cave hotels carved into cliffs, hot-air balloon rides at sunrise, and the deep cultural anchors of Konya (Mevlana / whirling dervishes) and Ankara (the country's modern capital).
regions.whenToGo: April–June and September–November (best balloon flight rates and clearest skies). Winter for snow on the fairy chimneys and 40% cheaper hotels.
highlights: Hot-air balloon flight at sunrise — Cappadocia's signature experience
Göreme Open-Air Museum — Byzantine cave churches with original frescoes
Derinkuyu underground city — 8 levels deep, sheltered 20,000 people
Uçhisar Castle viewpoint — sunset over all three valleys
Mevlana Museum in Konya — the Rumi tomb
Anıtkabir in Ankara — Atatürk's mausoleum
itinerary: Day 1–4: Cappadocia (Uçhisar or Göreme base) — balloon, valleys, underground cities
Day 5–6: Konya (Mevlana, whirling dervish ceremony if timed right)

regions.name: Black Sea Coast
regions.tagline: Tea plantations, alpine yaylas, the rainy green Turkey nobody photographs.
regions.summary: The Pontic Mountains drop straight into the Black Sea — tea fields wrap the slopes, monasteries cling to cliffs, and the high-altitude yayla pastures (Ayder, Pokut, Sal) feel more like Switzerland than the Mediterranean. The Turkey that locals visit when they want to escape Istanbul or Antalya.
regions.whenToGo: June–September (dry-ish summer; Black Sea is wetter than the rest of Turkey). May for blooming tea fields. Avoid winter (snow blocks yayla roads).
highlights: Sumela Monastery — Byzantine cliff-built monastery near Trabzon
Uzungöl — alpine lake village, Turkey's most overphotographed mountain spot for a reason
Ayder + Pokut yaylas — wooden mountain houses, mist, fresh trout
Tea plantation walks in Rize — pickers in bright orange among green hills
Hamsi (Black Sea anchovies) — fried, eaten whole, with corn bread
itinerary: Day 5–7: Rize area + Ayder + Pokut yayla

regions.name: Eastern Anatolia & Mesopotamia
regions.tagline: The cradle of civilizations — Mardin, Şanlıurfa, Gaziantep — where Turkey meets Mesopotamia.
regions.summary: The southeast is where Turkey gets weirder, older, and more interesting. Mardin's stone city perched over the Mesopotamian plain. Şanlıurfa's sacred fish ponds and the 12,000-year-old Göbekli Tepe. Gaziantep's UNESCO-recognized food culture. The trip Turkey enthusiasts come back for after the standard route.
regions.whenToGo: March–May and October–November (hot dry summers, mild winters). Avoid July–August (45°C in Şanlıurfa).
highlights: Göbekli Tepe — the world's oldest known temple, 12,000 years old
Mardin old city — sandstone houses cascading down a hill over the plains
Balıklıgöl in Şanlıurfa — sacred carp pool, Halil-ur Rahman mosque
Gaziantep food — UNESCO Creative City of Gastronomy, real baklava + kebabs + copperware
Diyarbakır's basalt walls — the longest continuous fortifications after the Great Wall (if visiting)
itinerary: Day 1–2: Gaziantep — food, copper bazaar, Zeugma Mosaics museum
Day 3–4: Şanlıurfa — Göbekli Tepe, Balıklıgöl, bazaars
Day 5–7: Mardin — old city, day trips to Hasankeyf, Midyat
